chunks.text: That day I hesitated in front of a small shop.
Should I buy the red gloves, or not?
It was a moment of quick decision.
Now I think I should have bought them.
I ought to have made my decision earlier, in time for my sister's birthday.
The next day I returned, but the gloves were already gone.
The shopkeeper said, " Yesterday another customer happened to purchase them.
I did not confess my regret to my sister.
She seemed not to mind and simply smiled at me kindly.
Perhaps I should have been more honest.
But she had already forgiven me.
Inside that small emotion, her mature nature appeared.
A person may regret, yet be saved by each other's affection.
